title: Noise: A Flaw in Human Judgment
author: Daniel Kahneman, Olivier Sibony, Cass R. Sunstein
excerpt: Wherever humans make judgments—from courtrooms to boardrooms, medical clinics to hiring committees—unwanted variability called 'noise' produces errors just as damaging as bias, yet it remains largely invisible. Kahneman, Sibony, and Sunstein reveal how to recognize noise and reduce it through decision hygiene.
